source_take: Letterboxd UI feels like a cluttered social network.
content:
SLIDE 1: [Hook — "Letterboxd was great before it turned into a cluttered social feed of hot takes."]
SLIDE 2: [Context — "I didn't want another feed to scroll. I just wanted a clean, fast way to track what I watch."]
SLIDE 3: [App screenshot — minimal movie tracking UI]
SLIDE 4: [App screenshot — customized taste profile]
SLIDE 5: [The insight — "Vista strips away the social noise. It's a premium, minimalist iOS experience designed for personal tracking, not public performance."]
SLIDE 6: [CTA — "Vista is live. Link in bio."]
CAPTION: pov: you just want to track movies without the noise
HASHTAGS: #iosapp #movielog #letterboxd #minimalistui #filmtok
score_notes: Hits the core UI/UX differentiation against Letterboxd directly. No IMDb or rating average talk.
